title: Rich Smoky Dutch Sweetcorn Stew
description: A smoky and velvety one-pot Dutch dish featuring sweetcorn, this pescatarian-friendly stew is perfect for a cold winter's night. It combines the natural sweetness of corn with the depth of smoky flavors.
instructions: Heat a large Dutch oven over medium heat, adding the smoked paprika to infuse the oil.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the sweetcorn, cod fillets, chicken broth, salt, and black pepper, stirring to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes or until the fish is cooked through.
Stir in the heavy cream and continue to simmer for an additional 2 minutes or until the stew has thickened slightly.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley.
Blanch any additional vegetables, such as carrots or green beans, and serve on the side if desired.
